heading: Demonstration grant program for local governments
label: 7628 Demonstration grant program for local governments
notes: Effective DateSection effective on the date that is 1 day after , see , set out as a note under , The Congress.Dec. 19, 2007section 1601 of Pub. L. 110–140section 1824 of Title 2

children.label: A
children.text: to deploy cost-effective technologies and practices; and

children.label: B
children.text: to achieve operational cost savings, through the application of cost-effective technologies and practices, as verified by the Administrator.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Federal share of the cost of an activity carried out using a grant provided under this section shall be 40 percent.

children.heading: Waiver of non-Federal share
children.label: B Waiver of non-Federal share
children.text: The Administrator may waive up to 100 percent of the local share of the cost of any grant under this section should the Administrator determine that the community is economically distressed, pursuant to objective economic criteria established by the Administrator in published guidelines.

children.heading: Maximum amount
children.label: 3 Maximum amount
children.text: The amount of a grant provided under this subsection shall not exceed $1,000,000.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , the Administrator shall issue guidelines to implement the grant program established under subsection (a).December 19, 2007

children.label: A
children.text: standards for monitoring and verification of operational cost savings through the application of cost-effective technologies and practices reported by grantees under this section;

children.label: B
children.text: standards for grantees to implement training programs, and to provide technical assistance and education, relating to the retrofit of buildings using cost-effective technologies and practices; and

children.label: C
children.text: a requirement that each local government that receives a grant under this section shall achieve facility-wide cost savings, through renovation of existing local government buildings using cost-effective technologies and practices, of at least 40 percent as compared to the baseline operational costs of the buildings before the renovation (as calculated assuming a 3-year, weather-normalized average).

children.heading: Compliance with State and local law
children.label: c Compliance with State and local law
children.text: Nothing in this section or any program carried out using a grant provided under this section supersedes or otherwise affects any State or local law, to the extent that the State or local law contains a requirement that is more stringent than the relevant requirement of this section.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: d Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section $20,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2007 through 2012.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Administrator shall provide annual reports to Congress on cost savings achieved and actions taken and recommendations made under this section, and any recommendations for further action.

children.heading: Final report
children.label: 2 Final report
children.text: The Administrator shall issue a final report at the conclusion of the program, including findings, a summary of total cost savings achieved, and recommendations for further action.

children.heading: Termination
children.label: f Termination
children.text: The program under this section shall terminate on .September 30, 2012

children.heading: Definitions
children.label: g Definitions
children.text: In this section, the terms “cost-effective technologies and practices” and “operating cost savings” shall have the meanings defined in .1section 17061 of this titleSo in original. Probably should be “operational”.1
